sections.title: Starting off
Talking to her again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, please find my darling! I just know something horrible has happened!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, thank Armadyl! I am in such a worry... please help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With what?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... it is terrible... horrible... My boyfriend lives here in this lighthouse, but I haven't seen him the last few days! I think something terrible has happened!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Look, you can see for yourself that the light has gone out, and the front door is locked up tight! He would NEVER do that!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: With the light off this coastline is terribly dangerous to ships! And to lock the front door so that nobody can turn the light back on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he just went on holiday or something? Must be pretty boring living in a lighthouse.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: That is terribly irresponsible! He is far too thoughtful for that! He would never leave it unattended! He would also never leave without telling me!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Please, I know something terrible has happened to him... I can sense it! Please... please help me adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how can I help?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Well, we have to do something to get the lighthouse working again! Also, as you may have noticed, the storm that knocked the bridge out

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: has trapped me on this causeway! You seem to have got here okay somehow, so if you could go and visit my cousin and get the spare key I left him,

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: as well as fix the bridge enough so that I can go and speak to my family in Rellekka and tell them whats happened, I will be eternally grateful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll help!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: OH! THANK YOU SO MUCH! I know my darling would never have left with the lighthouse lights off and without even telling me where he's gone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is your cousin?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My cousin was always interested in agility. He left our home in Rellekka many moons ago, so that he could pursue this interest.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I don't exactly know where he has gone, but I am sure he went somewhere to practise his agility. If you see him, his name is Gunnjorn. Mention my name, he will recognise it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I fix the bridge?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Well, I am not just some helpless girl! I have pretty good agility, so you will only need to use two planks to make a ledge that I can balance along.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Just use a plank on each side of the bridge. You will need a hammer, and thirty steel nails for each plank you use as well. I believe there are some planks near here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, just passing through.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... my poor darling Jossik... I just know something terrible has happened...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, just passing through.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... my poor darling Jossik... I just know something terrible has happened...

sections.title: Getting the key
After obtaining the key

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Welcome again to my obstacle course. Have fun, but remember this isn't a child's playground. People have died here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Gunnjorn. I'm afraid I've lost that key you gave me.

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Well, I would give you another, but apparently you don't have any room.

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: No problem. Here you go.

sections.title: Unknown condition

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, please find my darling! I just know something horrible has happened!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: What? My cousin is the one who gave you my spare copy of the lighthouse key. Do you have memory problems, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... no... I was just... Testing you. Yeah. Testing.

sections.title: Returning the key to Larrissa before repairing the bridge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your key for you!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Thank you adventurer, but I need you to fix the bridge for me. The key to the lighthouse is of little comfort while I am trapped here on this causeway!

sections.title: Returning to Larrissa after repairing the bridge but without the key

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed the bridge for you!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Please find the key to the lighthouse for me though!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I cannot bear to think that something bad may have happened to my darling Jossik...

sections.title: Unlocking the lighthouse with the lighthouse key
Attempting to enter the lighthouse before repairing the bridge

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Please adventurer... We are both curious as to what has happened in that lighthouse, but you need to fix the bridge for me!

sections.title: Using any non-plank item on the bridge before it is repaired
Repairing one side of the bridge
Repairing the second side of the bridge

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Haha welcome to my obstacle course. Have fun, but remember this isn't a child's playground. People have died here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, are you called Gunnjorn?

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Why, indeed I am. I own this agility course, it can be very dangerous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, that's great. Anyway, I understand you have a cousin named Larrissa who gave you a key...?

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Yes, she did! How do you know of this? She said she probably wouldn't need it, but gave it to me for safe keeping just in case.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, something has happened at the lighthouse, and she has been locked out. I need you to give me her key.

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Well, I would give you the key, but apparently you don't have any room.

lines.speaker: Gunnjorn
lines.text: Sure. Here you go.

sections.title: Returning with the key or door unlocked and the bridge repaired
Talking to her again before going inside

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Quickly, we must go inside and find out what has happened to my beloved Jossik!

sections.title: Attempting to go down the iron ladder before talking to her inside

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got your key for you!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Quickly, we must go inside and find out what has happened to my beloved Jossik!

sections.title: Talking to Larrissa inside the lighthouse
Talking to Larrissa again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: You MUST find a way to fix the lighthouse! The ships are in terrible danger as long as the light is not working properly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how I could do that?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sorry adventurer, I do not know anything about lighthouses.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sure there must be some kind of user's guide around here somewhere though.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My darling Jossik knew nothing about Lighthouses when he first came here, so the council must have left him some kind of manual or something.

sections.title: Talking to Larrissa outside the lighthouse before repairing the lighting mechanism

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Adventurer! You MUST do something about the broken light!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: It is terribly important that ships coming round this coastline do not crash here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I will see what I can do.

sections.title: Attempting to climb down the iron ladder before fixing the lighthouse

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Please adventurer... Do not let curiosity get the better of you! We Have to fix the lighthouse before any accidents happen!

sections.title: Searching the bookcase in the 1st floor[UK]2nd floor[US]
Using swamp tar on the lighting mechanism
Talking to Larrissa after using swamp tar on the mechanism

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: You MUST find a way to fix the lighthouse! The ships are in terrible danger as long as the light is not working properly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how I could do that?

lines.speaker: Larrissa

lines.speaker: Larrissa

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My darling Jossik knew nothing about Lighthouses when he first came here, so the council must have left him some kind of manual or something.

sections.title: Using a tinderbox on the lighting mechanism
Talking to Larrissa after using the tinderbox on the mechanism

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: You MUST find a way to fix the lighthouse! The ships are in terrible danger as long as the light is not working properly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how I could do that?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sorry adventurer, I do not know anything about lighthouses.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sure there must be some kind of user's guide around here somewhere though.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My darling Jossik knew nothing about Lighthouses when he first came here, so the council must have left him some kind of manual or something.

sections.title: Using molten glass on the lighting mechanism
Talking to Larrissa after using molten glass on the mechanism

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: You MUST find a way to fix the lighthouse! The ships are in terrible danger as long as the light is not working properly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any idea how I could do that?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sorry adventurer, I do not know anything about lighthouses.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I'm sure there must be some kind of user's guide around here somewhere though.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My darling Jossik knew nothing about Lighthouses when he first came here, so the council must have left him some kind of manual or something.

sections.title: After repairing the lighthouse torch

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: This is terrible...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: What can have happened here? Please, you must fix the light, we cannot let my darling Jossik take the blame for any shipwrecks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I will see what I can do.

sections.title: Returning to Larrissa
Talking to Larrissa outside

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Have you found any sign of my darling Jossik yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I came out here to see if I could find some sign of him...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: But there is nothing at all. I am so worried...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm sure he's fine. I'll find him somehow.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, thank you so much!

sections.title: Using a fire, water, air, or earth rune on the strange wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'll get that back if I put it in there.

sections.title: Using a valid arrow on the strange wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'll get that back if I put it in there.

sections.title: Using a valid sword on the strange wall
Talking to Larrissa after starting to put things in the strange wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have managed to fix the light!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'll get that back if I put it in there.

sections.title: After everything has been put into the strange wall
Talking to Larrissa after putting evrything in the strange wall

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have managed to fix the light!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Now you can devote all of your energies to finding out what has happened to my darling Jossik!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have managed to fix the light!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Excellent work, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Now you can devote all of your energies to finding out what has happened to my darling Jossik!

sections.title: Talking to Jossik in the basement

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: *cough* Please... please help me... I think my leg is broken, and those creatures will be back any minute now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're Jossik then... What creatures are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: I... I do not know. I have never seen their like before!

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: I was searching for information about my uncle Silas, who vanished mysteriously from this lighthouse many months ago. I found the secret of that strange wall, and discovered that I could use it as a door, but when I

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: came down here I was attacked by...

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: Well, I do not know what they are, but they are very strong! They hurt me badly enough to trap me here, and I have been fearing for my life ever since!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm here now. Larrissa was worried about you and asked for my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go back upstairs and let her know that I've found you and that you're still alive, and then we can work out some way of getting you out of here, okay?

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: NO! No, you can't leave me now! Look! They're coming again! Do something!

sections.title: Upon killing the dagannoth or talking to Jossik
Talking to Larrissa after killing the dagannoth

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Jossik! He's in a cavern underground...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: That is wonderful news! But why does he not come up out of there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... well, there's a big monster down there with him stopping him leaving.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: What?!?! You MUST save him! I beseech you, please! Do something!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, sheesh, keep your hair on...

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to blue

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Krrrrrrk

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to orange

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Chkhkhkhkhk

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to brown

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Krrrrrrssssssss

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to red

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Sssssrrrkkkkk

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to green

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Krkrkrkrkrkrkrkr

sections.title: When dagannoth mother changes to white

lines.speaker: Dagannoth mother
lines.text: Tktktktktktkt

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: NO! No, you can't leave me now! Look! They're coming again! Do something!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, now that the creature's dead we can get you out of here.

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: No... you do not understand...

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: That was not the creature that attacked me...

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: That was one of its babies...

sections.title: Upon killing the dagannoth mother

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, it's dead! Let's get out of here!

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: Yes, quickly, the mother might be dead, but it's children are not!

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: Follow me upstairs, I might be able to help you with that casket you found.

lines.speaker: Jossik
lines.text: Bring it to my library, it looks familiar somehow...
